reference_resolution.declared_rate_note: refs.yaml marks 22/23 refs resolvable=true (R04, a Roche Diagnostics method-sheet with no DOI, is the sole resolvable=false). Of the 22 resolvable refs, 12 were sampled here (capped at 12 per instructions); all 12 resolved via PubMed DOI lookup, matching the title cited in refs.yaml raw field in every case.

details.title: Alzheimer's disease: the amyloid cascade hypothesis.
Donanemab in Early Symptomatic Alzheimer Disease: The TRAILBLAZER-ALZ 2 Randomized Clinical Trial.
Donanemab exposure-response in early symptomatic Alzheimer's disease.
Amyloid-Related Imaging Abnormalities With Donanemab in Early Symptomatic Alzheimer Disease: Secondary Analysis of the TRAILBLAZER-ALZ and ALZ 2 Randomized Clinical Trials.
Temporal Dynamics of β-Amyloid Accumulation in Aging and Alzheimer Disease.
Quantifying natural amyloid plaque accumulation in the continuum of Alzheimer's disease using ADNI.
Establishing Clinically Meaningful Change on Outcome Assessments Frequently Used in Trials of Mild Cognitive Impairment Due to Alzheimer's Disease.
Assessing what matters most to patients with or at risk for Alzheimer's and care partners: a qualitative study evaluating symptoms, impacts, and outcomes.
The effect of modified donanemab titration on amyloid-related imaging abnormalities with edema/effusions and amyloid reduction: 18-month results from TRAILBLAZER-ALZ 6.
Association of Amyloid Reduction After Donanemab Treatment With Tau Pathology and Clinical Outcomes: The TRAILBLAZER-ALZ Randomized Clinical Trial.
Donanemab Population Pharmacokinetics, Amyloid Plaque Reduction, and Safety in Participants with Alzheimer's Disease.

claims.claim_text: Early-start donanemab slowed CDR-SB progression vs weighted ADNI external control by -1.2 points (95% CI -1.7,-0.7) at 3 years (36 months).
claims.rationale: PubMed searches for 'donanemab CDR-SB long-term extension propensity ADNI external control' and 'donanemab external control ADNI weighted comparator CDR-SB efficacy long-term' returned zero prior hits. This 3-year, ADNI-external-control-referenced efficacy estimate is unique to this LTE report; the pivotal 76-week trial (Sims 2023, PMID 37459141) used an internal placebo arm, not an ADNI comparator, and did not extend to 3 years.

claims.claim_text: Delayed-start donanemab slowed CDR-SB progression vs weighted ADNI control by -0.8 points (95% CI -1.3,-0.3) 76 weeks after donanemab initiation.
claims.rationale: Same search as C01; no prior article reports a delayed-start-vs-ADNI-control CDR-SB comparison at this specific post-initiation timepoint.

claims.claim_text: Early-start participants had 27% lower risk of CDR-Global progression vs delayed-start (HR=0.73 [0.62,0.86], p<0.001) over 3 years / 154 weeks.
claims.rationale: A related but non-identical CDR-Global progression-risk analysis exists in the donanemab literature (Atri et al., 'Clinical Meaningfulness of Donanemab...', PMID 42128444, pub. 2026-05-13: HR=0.67 MCI->mild AD, HR=0.50 mild->moderate AD, over the 76-week placebo-controlled period). However that article postdates this ARA's paper (tjpad.2025.100446, 2025) and uses a different timeframe/comparator (76-week placebo-controlled vs this paper's 154-week early- vs delayed-start LTE comparison), so it is not 'prior' work and does not report the same HR=0.73 finding. No genuinely prior (pre-2025) article reporting a CDR-Global early-vs-delayed-start hazard ratio in donanemab LTE data was found. Classified incremental rather than novel because the general technique (Cox PH on CDR-Global staging in donanemab trials) was already established by the time this paper published, even though the specific 154-week HR is new.

claims.claim_text: Amyloid plaque reduction robust and similarly sized regardless of early-/delayed-start timing (86.96 CL vs 86.01 CL at matched 76 weeks post-initiation); >75% achieved clearance (<24.1 CL) by 76 weeks post-initiation.
claims.rationale: High amyloid clearance rates (~80%) by 76 weeks with donanemab were already established in the pivotal TRAILBLAZER-ALZ 2 76-week trial report (Sims et al. 2023, PMID 37459141, JAMA). This LTE paper extends the finding to the delayed-start group at a matched post-initiation timepoint and confirms similar magnitude -- an incremental confirmation/extension rather than a new phenomenon.

claims.claim_text: Combining LTE data with three earlier donanemab studies, amyloid reaccumulation after discontinuation estimated at 2.4 CL/year, comparable to natural-history accumulation.
claims.rationale: Search 'amyloid reaccumulation rate Centiloid per year natural history model Alzheimer' returned zero hits, and 'amyloid plaque reaccumulation after donanemab discontinuation' returned only Shcherbinin 2022 (PMID 36094645, already a cited ref, R12, which reports amyloid reduction/clearance but not a reaccumulation rate). The specific 2.4 CL/year pooled reaccumulation estimate appears first in this LTE report.

claims.claim_text: No new safety signals observed during the LTE vs the established donanemab safety profile.
claims.rationale: This is a study-specific descriptive safety conclusion about the LTE period of this particular trial; by definition no prior article could report on 'this LTE's safety data' before this paper. Search 'donanemab ARIA safety long term extension' returned zero results, consistent with no prior LTE-specific safety report existing, but this is not a claim type that PubMed search can adjudicate as novel/incremental/previously_reported in the usual sense.

registry.trial_title: Assessment of Safety, Tolerability, and Efficacy of Donanemab in Early Symptomatic Alzheimer's Disease (TRAILBLAZER-ALZ 2)

concordance.outcome: CDR-SB change from baseline (registered secondary, Overall Population)
concordance.note: CDR-SB is a registered secondary outcome measure; this ARA reports it, but at post-hoc timepoints (36 months / 76wk-post-initiation) and against a post-hoc external control (ADNI) not specified in the original registration, which specifies Week 76 vs internal placebo.

concordance.outcome: Brain Amyloid Plaque Deposition by PET (registered secondary)
concordance.note: Amyloid PET Centiloid reduction is a registered secondary outcome; reported here at LTE timepoints (76wk post-init for each start group) beyond the original Week-76 registered timeframe.

'CDR-Global' as a distinct staged-progression/Cox-PH hazard-ratio endpoint does not appear in the registered primary or secondary outcome measures list for NCT04437511 (which lists iADRS, MMSE, ADAS-Cog13, CDR-SB, ADCS-iADL, amyloid PET, tau PET, vMRI, PK, ADA). The paper itself states (§2.5, §4) that all analyses were exploratory and not controlled for multiplicity.

concordance.outcome: Amyloid reaccumulation rate post-discontinuation, pooled 3-study model (C10)
concordance.note: Not a registered outcome measure; a modeling exercise combining this trial's LTE data with three other donanemab studies, explicitly exploratory per the paper's own multiplicity disclaimer.
Not a registered outcome measure; a derived post-hoc summary statistic computed from CDR-SB trajectories.

concordance.outcome: Tau PET, vMRI, PK, anti-drug antibody registered secondary outcomes
concordance.note: Registered as secondary outcomes for the main 76-week trial but not reported/discussed in this LTE-focused ARA (paper's scope is CDR-SB/CDR-Global efficacy, amyloid trajectory, and descriptive safety only).

retractions.notes: Searched PubMed for 'TRAILBLAZER-ALZ 2 retraction of publication', 'Sims donanemab TRAILBLAZER-ALZ 2 retraction correction', 'Sims donanemab TRAILBLAZER-ALZ 2 randomized clinical trial correction', and '37459141 AND Retraction of Publication[Publication Type]' -- all returned zero hits. No retraction or correction found for this paper (zim25, doi 10.1016/j.tjpad.2025.100446) or for its most-cited reference, the pivotal trial report (Sims et al. 2023, PMID 37459141, JAMA).

notes: All 23 refs in refs.yaml were inspected; 22 are marked resolvable=true (only R04, a non-DOI Roche method sheet, is resolvable=false). Per instructions, 12 of the resolvable refs were sampled for PubMed verification; all 12 resolved with titles matching refs.yaml's raw citation text exactly (observed_resolvability=1.0 on the sampled set vs the sidecar's implied declared rate of 22/23=0.957 across all refs -- consistent, no discrepancy found). Trial registry check confirms NCT04437511 is the correct pivotal trial (title, sponsor Eli Lilly, enrollment 1736, matches paper's Trial registration statement). Outcome-switching check found no primary-outcome switching, but did surface post-hoc/exploratory elevation of the CDR-Global hazard-ratio analysis and the amyloid-reaccumulation model to headline-claim status despite neither being a registered outcome measure -- consistent with, and explicitly disclosed by, the paper's own §2.5/§4 statement that all LTE analyses are exploratory and uncontrolled for multiplicity. Novelty check found no genuinely prior (pre-2025) literature reporting the specific 3-year ADNI-external-control CDR-SB deltas, the 154-week CDR-Global HR=0.73, or the 2.4 CL/year reaccumulation estimate; the amyloid-clearance-by-76-weeks finding (C07) is judged incremental on the already-published Sims 2023 76-week trial result.
